message:
fix(ci): add consistency fixes to scan workflow error handling

- Add -s check to validate file is non-empty before jq parsing
- Add 2>/dev/null || true to suppress jq errors on malformed input
- Consistent with validation in the summary generation step

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4","title":"Doctor: allowlist discovery + validation checks","description":"## Objective

Extend `dcg doctor` to validate allowlist installation and health once allowlists exist.

This is intentionally split out so the rest of `dcg doctor` can ship earlier.

## Requirements

- Detect user allowlist (`~/.config/dcg/allowlist.toml`) and project allowlist (`.dcg/allowlist.toml`).
- For each allowlist found:
  - validate schema version
  - validate required fields (`rule`, `reason`, etc.)
  - detect expired entries and surface as warnings
  - detect unsafe wildcard entries and surface as warnings/errors per allowlist safety rules

## Output UX

- In human-readable mode, print a clear status line per layer:
  - `User allowlist: OK` / `MISSING` / `INVALID` + 1-line remediation
  - `Project allowlist: OK` / ...

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor reports allowlist problems without crashing.
- Doctor guides users to:
  - `dcg allowlist validate --project|--user`
  - `dcg allowlist add ...`

## Tests

- Fixture allowlist files for:
  - valid entry
  - invalid schema
  - expired entry
  - unsafe wildcard missing `risk_ack`
- Unit tests assert doctor classification matches fixtures.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.2` (allowlists) must exist first.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:25.667664777-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:25.667664777-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:35.204041564-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:13:40.356612088-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.7.5","title":"Doctor: detect observe-mode expiry and recommend tightening","description":"## Objective

Prevent a common rollout failure mode: users enable warn-first observe mode and forget to tighten.

`dcg doctor` should detect when observe mode is enabled (and/or expired) and provide clear guidance.

## Requirements

- If `policy.default_mode` is warn/log-only globally, doctor prints:
  - current mode
  - whether an `observe_until` is set
  - whether it is expired

- If expired:
  - doctor warns prominently
  - prints a suggested config change to tighten (or remove observe)

## Acceptance criteria

- Doctor output makes it obvious when the guard is not enforcing.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.3.3` (observe mode config)

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","title":"Add dcg simulate (replay command logs for safe rollout + tuning)","description":"## Objective

Add a *simulation/replay* CLI mode so users can evaluate DCG policy on historical command streams **before** enforcing it.

This directly supports two real-world adoption problems:

1) Teams want to roll out in warn-only / observe mode first.
2) False positives are easier to eliminate when we can analyze a corpus offline.

## Key user stories

- "Before I enable strict packs, show me what would be blocked on my last 7 days of shell history."
- "Run on CI logs and summarize which rules would have fired most often."
- "Generate an allowlist skeleton for the highest-frequency false positives (with guardrails)."

## Proposed CLI

- `dcg simulate --file <path> [--format pretty|json] [--limit N] [--since ...]`
- Input auto-detect (per-line):
  - plain command string
  - hook JSON (`{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"..."}}`)
  - structured decision log entries (once `git_safety_guard-1gt.4` exists)

## Output (pretty)

- Summary counts: allow / warn / deny
- Top N rules (rule_id) by frequency
- Top files/contexts (if present in input)
- Optional: suggested next actions (enable pack, add allowlist, run explain)

## Output (json)

- Stable schema with:
  - totals
  - per-rule counts
  - per-pack counts
  - sample exemplars per rule (redacted/truncated)

## Safety / privacy

- Never print full commands by default in summaries; use truncation.
- Optional `--redact` consistent with scan/explain.

## Acceptance criteria

- Simulation can process large logs without panicking.
- Results are deterministic.
- Users can identify the top false-positive offenders quickly.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` shared evaluator (single source of truth)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.2` stable rule ids
- (optional) `git_safety_guard-1gt.4` structured log schema for one input mode
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4` should cover integration tests with:
  - streamed JSON log parsing
  - deterministic aggregation output
  - redaction/truncation
- Include at least one fixture demonstrating false-positive tuning (many warns, few errors).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:14:39.174102664-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:29:36.630036328-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:14:49.475594368-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:14:54.661067027-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:14:59.818843266-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1","title":"Simulate: input formats + streaming parser","description":"## Objective

Implement robust parsing for `dcg simulate` inputs with conservative auto-detection.

## Supported input line formats

1) **Plain command**
- The entire line is the command string.

2) **Hook JSON**
- `{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"..."}}`
- Ignore non-Bash tool events (treat as allow).

3) **Structured decision log entries** (post `git_safety_guard-1gt.4`)
- Parse log schema version and extract the command.

## Requirements

- Streaming: process the file line-by-line (avoid loading entire file).
- Limits:
  - `--max-lines`
  - `--max-bytes`
  - `--max-command-bytes`
- Error handling:
  - malformed lines are counted and reported, but do not abort (unless `--strict`).

## Acceptance criteria

- Parser never panics on arbitrary input lines.
- Auto-detection is deterministic (same line → same format classification).

## Tests

- Unit tests with fixture lines for each format.
- Fuzz target may be added later via `git_safety_guard-7tg.2`.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","title":"Simulate: evaluation loop + aggregation (counts, top rules)","description":"## Objective

Implement the core simulation loop that:

- evaluates each parsed command with the shared evaluator
- aggregates results into actionable summaries

## Aggregations (v1)

- totals: allow / warn / deny
- per-rule counts (`rule_id`)
- per-pack counts (`pack_id`)
- exemplar sampling per rule (keep top K shortest / first K occurrences)

## Determinism

- Sorting of summary outputs must be deterministic (count desc then rule_id asc).
- Sampling must be deterministic (first K occurrences by file order).

## Acceptance criteria

- `dcg simulate` produces stable output given the same input file.
- Aggregation respects limits (`--limit`, `--top N`, etc.).

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1` (parser)
- `git_safety_guard-99e.3.1` (shared evaluator)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Tests
- Aggregation output must be deterministic:
  - stable sorting for “top rules” and “top packs”
  - stable bucketing for allow/warn/deny
- Unit tests should assert stable ordering and correct counts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:42.58033159-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:29:46.96506937-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:52.76100775-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:15:57.931365713-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","title":"Simulate: output formats (pretty/json) + redaction/truncation","description":"## Objective

Implement `dcg simulate` output formatting in both human and machine forms.

## Pretty output requirements

- Summary header (totals + elapsed time)
- Top rules table:
  - rule_id
  - count
  - decision (deny/warn)
  - 1-line reason (if available)
- Optional section: sample commands per top rule (truncated + redacted)

## JSON output requirements

- `schema_version`
- totals
- `rules[]`: `{ rule_id, pack_id, decision, count, reason?, examples[] }`
- `errors[]`: parse errors counts, malformed lines count

## Privacy

- Default to truncation in examples.
- Support `--redact` consistent with scan/explain.

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is stable for golden tests.
- JSON output is valid and additive-only for schema v1.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2` (aggregation)
","notes":"## Review Additions (2026-01-08)

### Determinism
- Output formats must be deterministic and stable for diffing:
  - stable JSON schema versioning
  - stable sorting of aggregated tables
  - stable truncation/redaction rules","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:19.160687587-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T23:30:01.561272192-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:29.475130683-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:34.556618726-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","title":"Simulate: fixtures + integration tests + detailed logging","description":"## Objective

Add tests that make `dcg simulate` trustworthy and easy to evolve.

## Test strategy

### Unit tests

- parser unit tests (format detection) live with `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.1`.

### Integration tests (this task)

- Run the compiled binary with `dcg simulate` against fixture log files.
- Assert:
  - exit codes
  - key summary counts
  - top rule ordering stability
  - redaction/truncation behavior

## Logging requirements

Test failures should print:

- which fixture file
- which line number caused parse failure
- the offending line (truncated)
- the expected vs actual summary

## Acceptance criteria

- Tests are deterministic across runs.
- Adding a new fixture regression is easy.

## Dependencies

- `git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3` (output)
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:52.270612219-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T22:16:52.270612219-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:17:02.125735949-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-1gt.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T22:17:07.167409749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
@@ -52,7 +52,7 @@
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","title":"Fuzzing harness for high-risk parsers/tokenizers (cargo-fuzz)","description":"## Objective

Add fuzz targets for the components most likely to panic or hang:

- hook JSON parsing
- shell tokenizer / execution-context classifier
- heredoc extractor
- shared evaluator entrypoint

## Requirements

- Use `cargo-fuzz` (nightly) with time-limited CI runs.
- Add at least these fuzz targets:
  - `evaluate` (full evaluator)
  - `hook_input` (JSON parser)
  - `tokenizer` (context classifier)
  - `heredoc_extract`

## Acceptance criteria

- Fuzz targets run locally.
- CI runs fuzzing for a bounded time (e.g., 5 minutes per target on schedule).
- Any found crash becomes a regression test.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:43.859065882-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T00:54:58.127555188-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T00:54:58.127555188-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 4 fuzz targets (evaluate, hook_input, context, normalize). Fuzzing discovered a regex backtracking panic bug (git_safety_guard-hcj).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:13:14.712645803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:53.558806407-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:26:58.789818571-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-t8x.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:04.036438019-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","title":"Performance budgets + benchmark enforcement","description":"## Objective

Codify performance budgets and enforce them in CI so `dcg` stays invisible.

## Budgets to encode

- Fast path (no heredoc/inline): p99 under 500us
- Slow path (heredoc/inline): p99 under 20ms
- Absolute max: fail-open beyond 50ms

These numbers can be revised, but must be explicit.

## Requirements

- Benchmarks for:
  - quick reject / keyword gating
  - normalization
  - pack evaluation
  - overrides/allowlist
  - heredoc trigger/extract/AST path
- A CI job that compares benchmarks to budgets (tolerant of variance).

## Acceptance criteria

- Budgets exist as code constants/config.
- CI can detect large regressions and fail.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:49.116455774-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T22:05:28.941810806-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T22:05:28.941810806-05:00","close_reason":"Added CI benchmark enforcement: workflow job runs benchmarks on master push, checks against budgets in src/perf.rs, uploads results as artifacts","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:13:19.941175632-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-99e.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:09.296452284-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-perf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:14.532426804-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","title":"CI workflows: core suite vs deep suite (fuzz/proptest/bench)","description":"## Objective

Add CI workflows that run the right tests at the right cadence.

## Core suite (per PR)

- `cargo fmt --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test`
- selected E2E script coverage

## Deep suite (scheduled or manual)

- proptest ignored tests
- fuzzing time-limited runs
- benchmarks with budget checks

## Acceptance criteria

- CI is fast enough for PR iteration.
- Deep suite can be run without human babysitting.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:54.368764056-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T21:12:54.368764056-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:13:25.153093115-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:19.788712506-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:25.058948161-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:30.317207862-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.4","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-07T21:27:52.680215771-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"git_safety_guard-7tg.5","title":"Regression corpus harness (true/false positives + bypass attempts)","description":"## Objective

Create and maintain a small, curated corpus of high-signal command cases and a harness that runs them through:

- hook JSON path
- CLI evaluator path

## Corpus categories

- `true_positives/` (must block)
- `false_positives/` (must allow)
- `bypass_attempts/` (must block or warn)
- `edge_cases/` (must not crash/hang)

## Output/logging requirements

- Harness prints per-case:
  - description
  - expected decision
  - actual decision
  - rule id when matched

## Acceptance criteria

- Corpus harness runs in CI.
- Adding a new regression is easy and does not require writing Rust test code each time.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-8wt","title":"Create comprehensive test suite for heredoc detection","description":"## Objective

Create comprehensive test coverage for heredoc detection and AST-based pattern matching, including unit tests, integration tests, and bypass attempt tests.

## Test Categories

### 1. Unit Tests

#### Heredoc Detection Tests

- Bash heredoc variants: `<<EOF`, `<<-EOF`, `<<<`, `<<'EOF'`, `<<"EOF"`
- Python inline scripts: `python3 -c ...`
- Node.js: `node -e ...`, `node <<EOF`
- Perl: `perl -e ...`, `perl <<'END'`
- Ruby: `ruby -e ...`, heredoc variants

#### Language Detection Tests

- Detection from command prefix (python3, node, ruby, perl, bash)
- Detection from shebang (`#!/usr/bin/env python3`)
- Detection from content heuristics (bounded)
- Fallback behavior for unknown languages

#### Pattern Matching Tests

- Each destructive pattern per language (high-signal)
- Each safe/negative pattern (things that look dangerous but aren't)
- Edge cases in quoting and escaping

### 2. Integration Tests

#### Pipeline Tests

- Full flow from JSON input to block/allow decision
- Heredoc commands correctly trigger deep analysis
- Non-heredoc commands still use fast path
- Performance within latency budget

#### Error Handling Tests

- Malformed heredoc syntax
- Unparseable content
- Unknown languages fall back gracefully
- Timeout handling (fail-open in hook mode)

### 3. Bypass Attempt Tests

Curate a set of realistic bypass attempts we want to cover, e.g.:

- variable indirection (`CMD=rm; $CMD -rf ...`)
- command substitution (`$(echo rm) -rf ...`)
- `bash -c` nesting

(These should be weighted toward high-signal, common bypasses; avoid building a giant speculative list that creates noisy tests.)

### 4. False Positive Tests

Ensure we DON'T block legitimate uses:

- documentation containing command examples
- grep/rg patterns searching for dangerous commands
- `bd create` descriptions that mention dangerous patterns

### 5. Performance Tests

- Tier 1 trigger latency
- extraction latency
- AST match latency (bounded)
- large heredoc handling (size limits)

## Test infrastructure sketch

```
tests/
  fixtures/
    heredocs/
    bypass_attempts/
    false_positives/
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- Test suite is structured so failures are easy to attribute (tier1/extract/lang/match).
- Includes a curated corpus that covers:
  - main heredoc forms
  - at least one example per supported language
  - a small set of realistic bypass attempts
  - a strong false-positive regression set
- Enforces policy behaviors:
  - hook fail-open on parse/timeout
  - severity mapping (deny vs warn)
- Provides clear logs/assert messages so CI failures are actionable.

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e","title":"Epic: Core Pipeline Correctness & Determinism","description":"# Epic: Core Pipeline Correctness & Determinism

## Why this epic exists

`dcg` is only useful if it is **correct, predictable, and consistent**:

- Correct: enabled packs actually run when their keywords appear.
- Predictable: the same input yields the same allow/deny + the same reason.
- Consistent: hook-mode decisions match `dcg test` and any future "explain" tooling.

Right now the codebase has a critical architectural correctness gap:

- Hook mode short-circuits early when the top-level command contains no `git`/`rm` (`src/main.rs:587`).
- `global_quick_reject` also only checks `git`/`rm` (`src/packs/mod.rs:383`).

As a result, **non-core packs (docker/kubectl/databases/etc.) are effectively unreachable in real hook traffic**, even if enabled in config. This is a trust-destroying failure mode because the CLI (`dcg test`) *can* report a block while the hook *allows* the same command.

We also have a determinism gap:

- Pack evaluation order is currently based on iterating a `HashSet` (`src/packs/mod.rs:299+`), which is not stable.
- If multiple packs would block, the user-visible reason (and sometimes which pack is attributed) can vary run-to-run.

Finally, the project is mid-migration:

- Legacy pattern matching exists in `src/main.rs` and duplicates core pack patterns.
- Hook input parsing and denial output are duplicated between `src/main.rs` and `src/hook.rs`.

This epic fixes the above so future features (false-positive immunity, heredoc scanning, AST analysis, explainability) are built on a solid, deterministic foundation.

## Goals

1. **Pack reachability:** if an enabled pack’s keywords appear in the command, the pack is actually evaluated in hook mode.
2. **Deterministic decisions:** stable pack ordering + stable pattern identity in results.
3. **Single source of truth:** one hook parser and one allow/deny pipeline used by hook mode and CLI test mode.
4. **Performance preserved:** keep the hot path fast (microseconds) and avoid per-invocation regex compilation.
5. **Naming alignment:** eliminate `git_safety_guard`/`dcg` naming drift in env vars, scripts, docs, and output.

## Non-goals (for this epic)

- Adding new destructive patterns/categories (handled by the pack/library work).
- Backwards compatibility guarantees (early-stage project; prefer correctness and clarity).

## Success Criteria

- With `containers.docker` enabled, `docker system prune` is blocked in *hook mode* (stdin JSON) and in `dcg test`, with the same pack attribution and stable reason.
- With `kubernetes.kubectl` enabled, `kubectl delete namespace foo` is blocked similarly.
- Re-running the same blocked input 100 times yields identical `pack_id`/`pattern_name` attribution.
- The hook + CLI paths share one evaluator so parity is enforced by design.
- No per-command regex compilation for config overrides on the hot path.

## Notes for future self

- This epic must be completed **before** we trust any new pack work or any UX/"explain" features.
- If we later add a scoring system (severity/precision), this epic provides the deterministic substrate (stable ordering + stable pattern IDs).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-99e.1","title":"BUG: Non-core packs are unreachable in hook mode (global quick reject)","description":"## Summary

In hook mode, `dcg` returns early for any command that does not contain `git` or `rm`, which makes all non-core packs (docker/kubectl/databases/etc.) effectively unreachable in real Claude Code hook traffic.

This is a correctness bug and a trust issue because `dcg test` *can* report a block while the actual hook path silently allows.

## Steps to Reproduce

Prereq: enable a non-core pack (e.g., `containers.docker`) in config.

Input (hook JSON):
```json
{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"docker system prune"}}
```

- Expected: DENY (pack `containers.docker`, pattern `system-prune`).
- Actual (today): ALLOW due to early return at `src/main.rs:587` and `global_quick_reject` only checking `git`/`rm`.

## Root cause

- `src/main.rs:587`: `if quick_reject(&command) && global_quick_reject(&command) { return; }`
- `src/packs/mod.rs:383`: `global_quick_reject` only checks for substrings `git` and `rm`.

So any command lacking these substrings exits before `REGISTRY.check_command(...)` runs.

## Design constraints

- Must preserve hot-path performance for the 99% case.
- Must remain correct under arbitrary enabled pack sets.

## Proposed fix options

### Option A (recommended): pack-aware global quick reject

- After loading config, compute enabled packs.
- Build a fast keyword-gating check that returns “definitely safe to skip packs” only if the command contains **none** of:
  - core keywords (`git`, `rm`)
  - keywords for enabled packs (`docker`, `kubectl`, `psql`, etc.)

Implementation can start simple:
- iterate the enabled packs’ `Pack.keywords` and `memmem::find` for each keyword
- (optional later) replace with an Aho-Corasick automaton or `regex::RegexSet` for large keyword sets

### Option B: delete global quick reject, rely on per-pack `might_match`

- Always run `REGISTRY.check_command` (it already keyword-gates per pack).
- Might be slightly slower but likely still fine; measure.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. With `containers.docker` enabled, `docker system prune` is denied in hook mode.
2. With `kubernetes.kubectl` enabled, `kubectl delete namespace foo` is denied in hook mode.
3. With `database.postgresql` enabled, destructive SQL strings (if passed directly) are denied when appropriate.
4. `dcg test` and hook mode agree for the same command.
5. Add regression tests that would have caught this:
   - a unit/integration test that runs the hook pipeline on a JSON input and asserts denial for a non-git/rm command.

## Notes

This bug blocks meaningful work on most non-core packs until fixed.
